prompt:
The end-of-shift handover report our ops desk generates is coming out mangled, and the wiki importer now rejects it every night. handover.sh reads the shift's events TSV and writes the handover text. Here's what last night's run actually produced:

    SHIFT HANDOVER -- day-12\n
    OPEN ITEMS
    OPEN-DB\t2
    OPEN-NET\t1

    NOTES
    - 07:14  cooler alarm reset itself
    - 09:02  vendor called back re: dock 4

    Questions: page $oncall via the rota.

Three separate absurdities in one small file: the header line has a literal backslash-n glued onto it (and the blank line that's supposed to follow the header is gone), the OPEN ITEMS rows have a literal backslash-t where the importer needs an actual tab character between the code and the count, and the footer tells people to page someone named, literally, dollar-sign-oncall — the on-call's name is passed as the third argument and clearly isn't landing. The notes section, infuriatingly, is perfect.

Fix handover.sh so the report comes out the way it was obviously meant to: real blank line after the header, real tabs in the OPEN ITEMS rows, and the actual on-call name in the footer. Layout is load-bearing (the importer splits on tabs and blank lines), so don't rearrange anything else. bash test_shiftnote.sh byte-compares the output — it must pass unmodified, and leave the test file alone.
